name: FTC Endorsement Guides
summary: Federal guidance requiring disclosure of material connections between endorsers and advertisers.

evidence.claim: FTC Guides Concerning the Use of Endorsements and Testimonials in Advertising (16 CFR Part 255) were revised in 2023 to strengthen disclosure requirements for influencer marketing and affiliate content.

body.officialName: FTC Guides Concerning the Use of Endorsements and Testimonials in Advertising
body.scopeSummary: Requires clear and conspicuous disclosure of any material connection between an endorser and a brand — including monetary payments, free products, employment relationships, or affiliate commissions. Applies to social media, blog posts, video content, and customer reviews.
body.userRightsPlainLanguage: If an influencer, reviewer, or 'expert' recommends a product but was paid, given the product free, or earns a commission from your purchase, they are required to tell you so clearly. Tiny hashtags buried in a bio don't count. If you don't see a clear disclosure, the content is likely violating the FTC Endorsement Guides.

enforcementSignals.description: Report undisclosed sponsored content to the FTC.
